With no CCTV or eyewitnesses, investigators relied on Mary's keen sense of smell. She expertly tracked a scent from a recovered scarf, leading police directly to the accused's residence. The swift operation, culminating in the suspect's capture and confession, highlights the invaluable role of canine units in solving complex crimes.

After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.
